steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence already has a main verb: "analyzed." The blank must introduce a verb form that modifies "researchers" without creating a second competing main verb.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'were looking': A finite verb form; creates two main verbs without a conjunction. ✗
  • Choice B — 'looked': Also finite; same structural error. ✗
  • Choice C — 'looking': A participle — non-finite, so it modifies "researchers" while leaving "analyzed" as the sole main verb. ✓
  • Choice D — 'had looked': Finite past perfect; same two-main-verb problem. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
